2606.10499.title: MoE Enhanced Federated Learning for Spatiotemporal Prediction
2606.10499.paper_summary: Traffic prediction is fundamental to intelligent transportation systems and urban computing, yet many cities continue to suffer from traffic data scarcity due to limited sensor deployment and uneven urban development. Cross-city knowledge transfer has thus attracted increasing attention, enabling data-rich cities to assist data-scarce ones. However, centralized approaches raise privacy concerns, while existing federated methods struggle with pronounced spatiotemporal heterogeneity across cities. To address these challenges, we propose MoE-FedTP, a personalized federated cross-city spatiotemporal prediction framework based on lightweight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) networks. MoE-FedTP first employs spatiotemporal neural networks to extract features from both source and target cities, then introduces a set of expert networks derived from different source cities through partial parameter sharing. A gating mechanism dynamically fuses the experts to capture diverse traffic dynamics, achieving fine-grained modeling of urban heterogeneity while preserving privacy. Experiments on four real-world traffic datasets show that MoE-FedTP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art cross-city and federated learning baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness in enhancing prediction accuracy for data-scarce cities.
2606.10499.paper_summary_zh: 交通預測是智慧交通系統和城市計算的基礎，但許多城市仍然因為感測器部署有限和城市發展不均而遭受交通數據稀缺的困擾。因此，跨城市知識轉移吸引了越來越多的關注，使數據豐富的城市能夠協助數據稀缺的城市。然而，集中式方法引發了隱私問題，而現有的聯邦方法在城市之間的時空異質性上面臨挑戰。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 MoE-FedTP，一個基於輕量級專家混合模型（Mixture-of-Experts, MoE）網絡的個性化聯邦跨城市時空預測框架。MoE-FedTP 首先利用時空神經網絡從源城市和目標城市中提取特徵，然後引入一組通過部分參數共享從不同源城市衍生的專家網絡。一個閘控機制動態融合這些專家，以捕捉多樣的交通動態，實現對城市異質性的細緻建模，同時保護隱私。在四個真實世界交通數據集上的實驗表明，MoE-FedTP 始終超越最先進的跨城市和聯邦學習基準，證明了其在提高數據稀缺城市預測準確性方面的有效性。
2606.10499.author: Zhehao Dai et.al.
2606.10499.authors: Zhehao Dai, Xiao Han, Zhaolin Deng, Zijian Zhang, Xiangyu Zhao, Guojiang Shen, Xiangjie Kong
